title: Continual Reinforcement Learning Framework for Scalable Collision Avoidance and Mitigation System With Packing Strategy
authors: Joonhee Lim, Jangho Shin, Dongsuk Kum
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Collision Avoidance and Mitigation System (CAMS) in autonomous driving systems is crucial for ensuring safety by formulating strategies to address various collisions and planning the trajectory accordingly. Although recent learning-based motion planning methods for CAMS have shown promising results for specific collision scenarios, the question of how to continually scale up their knowledge across different driving environments has not yet been thoroughly investigated. In this paper, we propose a scalable learning framework for CAMS that combines deep reinforcement learning and continual learning. By sequentially packing knowledge of new scenarios into a model trained on specific scenarios, the proposed method continually scales up the range of scenarios the model can handle while maintaining performance on previously trained scenarios. We apply this learning framework to a motion planner for CAMS and evaluate its scalability across new driving environments. This approach demonstrates a significant ability to pack knowledge by outperforming a model that jointly trains on all scenarios—typically considered an upper bound for continual learning—in about 2.4× fewer training steps, with a 4.19% higher collision avoidance rate and a 12.02% lower collision impact. Moreover, our approach achieves performance close to models trained on each scenario individually, with a model about 5.8× smaller.

title: Impact of Sensor Placement and Gait Dynamics on Off-Body MBAN Channel Characteristics: A Comprehensive Performance Evaluation of BER and Data Throughput at 2.36-2.39 GHz
authors: Nada Elmaradany, Heba Shaban, Noureldin Elmadany
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Medical body area networks (MBANs) operating in the 2360–2390 MHz band enable continuous health monitoring across a wide range of clinical and home-care applications. This paper presents a comprehensive performance evaluation of off-body MBAN links based on Monte Carlo simulations using empirically derived channel models from the IEEE 802.15.6 standard. Two transmitter locations, chest mounted and right wrist mounted sensors, are investigated under standing and walking conditions for distances ranging from 1 m to 4 m and angular orientations of 0°, 90°, 180°, and 270°. The impact of sensor placement and gait dynamics on bit error rate (BER) and theoretical maximum achievable throughput is analyzed. Constraints and practical hardware limitations are considered by restricting transmit power to specific absorption rate (SAR) and by analyzing energy consumption using parameters from commercial wearable transceivers. The obtained Eb/N0 values range from 10 dB to 76 dB at n=2, while throughput varies between 68.5 bps and 270 Mbps depending on distance, activity, and orientation. High Eb/N0 values observed under severe fading conditions are shown to be impractical under realistic power and energy constraints. The results indicate that transmission distance is the dominant performance factor in static scenarios, whereas sensor placement becomes more important under dynamic conditions. In general, chest mounted sensors outperform wrist mounted sensors due to reduced body shadowing in the first case. Furthermore, the use of equal gain combining (EGC) is shown to overcome mobility induced performance degradation for chest mounted nodes, enabling reliable support of a 1 Mbps data rate in all considered scenarios considering the chest as the Tx. Robustness analysis under obstructed indoor propagation (n = 3.5) confirms that although absolute throughput decreases with higher path loss, the relative performance ranking between placement and mobility scenarios remains unchanged. These findings provide quantitative design guidance for reliable and energy efficient MBAN deployment in indoor healthcare environments.

title: scaleTRIM: Scalable TRuncation-Based Integer Approximate Multiplier with Linearization and Compensation
authors: Ebrahim Farahmand, Mohammad Javad Askarizadeh, Ali Mahani, Behnam Ghavami, Hassan Ghasemzadeh, Muhammad Abdullah Hanif, Muhammad Shafique
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, we propose a scalable approximate multiplier design, scaleTRIM, that approximates the multiplication operation using fitted linear functions, also referred to as linearization. We show that multiplication operations can be completely replaced by low-cost addition and bit-wise shift operations by exploiting linearization. Moreover, our proposed design utilizes a lookup table (LUT)-based compensation unit as a novel error-reduction method. In essence, input operands are truncated to a reduced bit-width representation (i.e., h bits) based on their leading-one positions. Then, a curve-fitting method is employed to map the product term to a linear function. Additionally, a piecewise constant error-correction term is used to reduce the approximation error. To compute the piecewise constant, we divide the function space into M segments and average the errors within each segment. In particular, our multiplier supports various degrees of truncation and error compensation to offer a range of accuracy-efficiency trade-offs. The proposed multiplier improves the Mean Relative Error Distance (MRED) by about 15.2% while satisfying the efficiency constraint and improves the Power Delay Product (PDP) by about 22.8% while satisfying the accuracy and efficiency constraints compared to different state-of-the-art approximate multipliers. From a usability perspective, our evaluation of the proposed design for image classification using Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) demonstrates that scaleTRIM offers a better accuracy-efficiency trade-off than state-of-the-art approximate multiplier designs.

title: A Reinforcement Learning Framework for UAV Path Planning Automation in Complex Fire and Rescue Environments
authors: Yanming Chen, Saeid Pourroostaei Ardakani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Computing
abstract: Fire and rescue UAVs employ AI methods to automate navigation over fire zones to detect and extinguish fire f lames and/or rescue fire-trapped lives through low-cost and collision-free flight routes. This paper introduces a reinforcement learning framework that allows UAVs to detect and prioritise fire trapped targets based on their risk levels. It establishes three simultaneous objectives, including maximised power conservation, rescue success rate, and flight safety, to plan fire and rescue operations in complex environments where field size, the number of trapped targets, and fire source count vary. An extensive empirical evaluation is conducted to test and evaluate the performance of the proposal against three well-known benchmarks, including Double Deep Q-network, Advantage Actor-Critic, and Genetic Algorithm. The results demonstrate that the proposed solution outperforms the benchmarks in most circumstances, especially when the fire and rescue environment is large and complex.

title: Activation Analyses in Support of the Integration of the ITER Diagnostic Equatorial Ports
authors: M. Lungaroni, D. Flammini, A. Colangeli, M. Damiano, S. Loreti, N. Fonnesu, F. Moro, S. Noce, A. Previti, V. Lombardi, R. Villari, S. Pavel, S. Soro, F. Curiano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) diagnostic equatorial port plugs (EPPs) are located in the vacuum vessel equatorial ports (EQs), directly facing the plasma, thus exposed to intense neutron irradiation produced by the fusion reactions. Neutron irradiation results in both heat deposition and long-term activation of port plug structural materials. This work presents activation analyses in support of the ITER Diagnostic EPPs #2, #8 and #12, which host different diagnostics systems. Three-dimensional neutronic analyses were performed by means of D1SUNED 3.1.4 rev.2 tool based on the Monte Carlo neutron particles (MCNP) Monte Carlo code, using detailed port plug models embedded in proper sectors of the ITER reference MCNP model. These simulations provided neutron flux energy spectra within the port plugs and their subcomponents, represented in 175 VITAMIN-J energy groups. The resulting spectra have been used as input for the activation calculations carried out with FISPACT-II inventory code, employing TENDL-2017 nuclear data libraries and the SA2 ITER reference operational scenario. Specific activity, decay heat, and contact dose rate of the port plugs components have been calculated after the machine shutdown for different cooling times, extending up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{{4}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> years. This article presents the activation analysis results for the components of the selected EPP, with a focus on time-dependent behavior, highlighting major similarities and differences in activation responses and characteristics.

title: Effects of Voltage Increase Rate on Paschen’s Curve in Air
authors: Giacomo Galli, Michael J. Kirkpatrick, Emmanuel Odic
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: Paschen’s law relates the gaseous dielectric breakdown voltage to the product of gas pressure and electrode separation distance. The literature contains studies that use different voltage rise times in experiments; theoretically, this should not pose a problem: the probability of finding a seed electron to initiate a discharge between two infinitely large electrodes is assumed to be 100%. However, in real systems, this is not the case, and thus, the rate of voltage increase becomes an important factor in the process. The goal of the present work was to assess the impact of different voltage rise rates on the observed discharge voltage in air, for various electrode geometries and gas pressures. Pressure is an important factor when using an electrode arrangement giving an inhomogeneous electric field because, as it decreases, the volume in which the reduced electric field is strong enough to ensure that the ionization rate exceeds the attachment rate increases, thus also increasing the probability of the presence of a seed electron. To facilitate this experimental investigation, a customized system operating at pressures between 1 mbar and 1 bar, at room temperature, and designed to test both plane–plane and point–plane electrode configurations with a fixed distance between electrodes was developed. Results from the experimental study on deviations from Paschen’s law under different voltage rise rates across varying electrode geometries and pressures will be presented, followed by a discussion of electrostatic simulations of the experimental systems, comparing these findings with the experimental results. Finally, the results will be discussed, and some possible interpretations will be outlined.

title: Advanced Optimization of THz Biosensor for Breast Cancer Detection Through Machine Learning
authors: Hamza Wertani, Hamza Ben Krid, Aymen Hlali, Hassen Zairi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This work presents a highly sensitive and reconfigurable terahertz (THz) biosensor for the detection of breast cancer biomarkers. The device consists of a hybrid graphene-gold THz biosensor patterned on a dielectric substrate, providing strong plasmonic field confinement and tunable resonance characteristics. To optimize its optical response, a support vector machine (SVM) regression model was employed to capture the nonlinear interaction among graphene’s chemical potential, relaxation time, and temperature. The optimized structure achieves excellent sensing performance, with sensitivities of 137.2 GHz/RIU for normal epithelium (NE), 193.7 GHz/RIU for CA15-3, 241.6 GHz/RIU for CA27-29, and 311.5 GHz/RIU for HER2, along with a quality factor (Q-Factor) of 6.66 and a figure of merit (FOM) of 636.7 RIU<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>. Combining tunability, compactness, and machine-learning-based optimization, the proposed hybrid graphene-gold THz biosensor demonstrates great promise for real-time, label-free, and precise breast cancer diagnostics.

title: Leveraging AI Agents for Autonomous Networks: A Reference Architecture and Empirical Studies
authors: Binghan Wu, Shoufeng Wang, Ya-Qin Zhang, Joseph Sifakis, Ye Ouyang
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The evolution toward Level 4 (L4) Autonomous Networks (AN) represents a strategic inflection point in telecommunications, where networks must transcend reactive automation to achieve genuine cognitive capabilities - fulfilling AN's vision of self-configuring, self-healing, and self-optimizing systems that deliver zero-wait, zero-touch, and zero-fault services. This work bridges the gap between architectural theory and operational reality by implementing Joseph Sifakis's AN Agent reference architecture in a functional cognitive system, deploying coordinated proactive-reactive runtimes driven by hybrid knowledge representation. Through an empirical case study of a Radio Access Network (RAN) Link Adaptation (LA) Agent, we validate this framework's performance. Specifically, the system demonstrates sub-10 ms real-time control in 5G NR sub-6 GHz environments. Empirical results show a 4% increase in downlink throughput over Outer Loop Link Adaptation (OLLA) algorithms for enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB). Furthermore, for the ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) scenario, the agent achieves an 85% reduction in Block Error Rate (BLER). These improvements confirm the architecture's viability in overcoming traditional autonomy barriers and advancing critical L4-enabling capabilities toward next-generation objectives.

title: CHAINOPT: Heterogeneity-aware Blockchain Performance Optimization for Dynamic Workloads
authors: Biqi Zhao, Yushan Zeng, Jiejie Zhao, Haiquan Wang, Shan Zhang, Lei Li, Haogang Zhu, Runhe Huang, Weifeng Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: As reliable distributed systems, Blockchains have been widely applied in diverse domains, such as the Internet of Things (IoT). Recent studies have explored Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to enhance blockchain performance. However, existing DRL-based blockchain performance optimization methods rely on implicit and idealized assumptions about node behaviors and transactional workloads, limiting their effectiveness and efficiency on the blockchain with dynamic work-loads and heterogeneous nodes. To alleviate this, we propose CHAINOPT, a novel blockchain performance optimization framework devised for optimal parameter configuration to handle dynamic workloads and heterogeneous nodes. Specifically, we first propose an interaction-aware state representation learning module to model both global system-level and local heterogeneous node feature interactions to generate better state representations. Then, a contrastive learning-enhanced workload identification module is designed to extract discriminative workload-specific state representations to improve workload identification accuracy. Finally, we design a workload-similarity guided policy reuse module to produce effective reuse weights to transfer knowledge from history policies based on workload relevance, thereby improving optimization speed and stability. Extensive experiments show the effectiveness of CHAINOPT in improving blockchain performance, achieving 185.87% higher scalability and 1492.16% stronger security with only a marginal 6.26% latency increase. Moreover, it outperforms baselines in static scenarios while maintaining considerable superiority under varying workloads.

title: What Are They Asking? Analyzing Multilingual Student Questions to ChatGPT in Computer Science Education
authors: Anna Kruspe, Daniel Moritz Marutschke
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT are increasingly used by students to support their learning, yet little is known about what kinds of questions students ask and how they engage with these tools, especially in multilingual contexts. This study analyzes around 600 messages collected from students in an introductory computer science courses taught to international liberal arts students at a Japanese university, where participants were invited to interact with ChatGPT in either English or their native language. Using language detection, sentiment analysis, topic modeling, and intent classification, we explore the linguistic choices, thematic focus, and emotional tone of student inquiries. Contrary to expectations, students rarely used ChatGPT for basic or factual questions—instead, they predominantly posed in-depth, exploratory questions and sought creative ways to apply course content—topics that might not fit within typical course interactions or where students may fear judgment. Our findings reveal a preference for English, a wide range of course-related topics, mostly neutral sentiment, and a notable tendency toward creative and advanced engagement. These insights shed light on how LLMs can foster deeper learning and creativity in multilingual computer science education, informing the design of supportive AI-integrated learning environments.

title: Boost-Integrated Dual-Bridge llc Resonant Converter with Fault-Tolerant Capability for Battery Charging Applications
authors: Krishna Velmajala, Srinivasa Rao Sandepudi
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a boost-integrated dual-bridge LLC resonant dc-dc converter with enhanced fault-tolerant capability, aimed to obtain higher voltage gain and high-reliability for battery charging applications. To maintain continuous operation in the presence of short-circuit or open-circuit faults in power switches, the conventional single-phase full-bridge architecture is reconfigured by integrating a boost mechanism and replacing the single primary winding transformer with a dual primary winding transformer structure to adopt an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{5}$</tex> configuration, enhancing fault resilience and electrical isolation. The proposed converter supports multiple operating modes designed to maintain zero-voltage switching (ZVS), thereby reducing switching losses and significantly improves overall efficiency and power density. A comprehensive fault analysis is performed with all possible fault modes. Extensive simulations under both pre-fault and post-fault conditions validate the converter reliability and effectiveness, demonstrating its robustness solution for battery charging applications.

title: Research on Robust Day-Ahead Dispatch for Dual-Type Demand Response in New Power Systems
authors: Shuaibin Yang, Hang Zhang, Genxu Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the expansion of wind power grid integration, the random fluctuations it introduces exert significant pressure on system peak regulation. Balancing operational safety with economic efficiency has become an urgent challenge in the field of dispatch. To address this, this paper proposes a robust day-ahead dispatch method for dual-type demand response tailored to new power systems. Regarding load-side response mechanisms, this paper employs a refined modelling approach for industrial flexible resources, categorising them into Continuously Regulable Loads (CRLs) and Discretely Regulable Loads (DRLs). CRLs utilise electricity price elasticity coefficients to describe their smooth power adjustment processes, while DRLs are characterised by binary variables depicting their discontinuous step-like behaviour. A source-load coordinated robust optimisation dispatch framework is established with the objective of minimising total system operating costs, solved using mixed-integer linear programming. Case study results demonstrate the model's effectiveness in mitigating risks arising from wind power forecasting errors. Compared to conventional models neglecting demand response, the coordinated participation of dual-type demand response reduces total system costs by 6.05%. The findings confirm that deeply tapping demand-side flexibility resources in conjunction with robust optimisation techniques represents an effective pathway to enhance wind power integration levels and economic operation capabilities within new power systems.

title: Fault Branch Location Method for Substation Grounding Grid Based on ICSA Algorithm
authors: Bin Peng, Hanjin Xue, Chenbin Gong, Zhi Li, Tianci Li, Guangmin Gao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the difficulty of fault location caused by branch corrosion in substation grounding grids, this paper proposes a precise positioning method based on an improved cuckoo search algorithm. Considering that the resistivity of Q235 steel conductors is much lower than that of soil, the influence of soil resistance is ignored, and the grounding grid is equivalent to a pure resistive circuit. Based on KCL and KVL, a mapping relationship between node voltages and branch resistances is established, and a fault diagnosis model with minimum energy loss is constructed by taking the branch resistance increase multiple as the optimization variable. To solve the problems of fixed step size, slow convergence speed, and limited search accuracy of the standard cuckoo search algorithm, an adaptive step size mechanism is introduced to dynamically adjust the scaling factor and discovery probability, optimizing the optimization performance of the algorithm. Through building a 60-branch and 60-branch and 36-node grounding grid simulation platform, verification is carried out under single-branch and multi-branch fault scenarios, and comparative experiments are conducted with the Co-evolution Genetic Algorithm (CGA), improved Artificial Bee Colony algorithm (YHABC). The results show that the diagnostic error of the proposed method is less than 10% with excellent stability, which can accurately identify fault branches. Moreover, it outperforms the comparative algorithms in terms of convergence speed and positioning accuracy, providing reliable technical support for the efficient operation, maintenance and inspection of grounding grids.

title: MPC-Based Six-Switch MFC Using Novel Bobcat Optimized Cascaded Control in a Grid-Interactive Micro Grid Operation
authors: Md Imran, Jyoti Prakash Mishra, Ravi Kumar Majji, Ashish A. Dongre
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper presents SPV and Wind Turbine Generator (WTG) integrated Six Switch Converter (SSC) based Single-Phase Multifunctional Converter (MFC) for compensating supply side different power quality issues while supplying renewable generated power to voltage sensitive critical loads. The SSC topology provides dual single-phase outputs with distinct electrical characteristics. One output terminal of the SSC is configured in series proximity to the point of common coupling (PCC), while the other is connected in shunt near the load terminal. The series configuration effectively compensates for supply voltage sags, swells, and harmonic distortions, whereas the shunt configuration ensures the delivery of generated power to the connected sensitive loads. The proposed MPC-based Fractional Order Proportional Resonant Proportional Integral (FOPR-PI) controller is designed for achieving a fast dynamic response of the grid system under various loaded conditions, source-side disturbances & system parameter variations. Bobcat Optimization Algorithm optimizes the cascaded controller to identify the optimal parameters of the controllers. The performance of the proposed system is validated through simulations conducted in MATLAB/Simulink at different operating conditions and disturbances.

title: A Review of Electrification Challenges with e-Aircraft for Consumer Potential Use
authors: Shiqi Zhou, Yujie Yuan, Zhekang Dong, Xiaoqing Zhong, Chun Sing Lai, Hongjie Jia, Siqi Bu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: As the global aviation industry transitions toward a low-carbon economy, electric aircraft, with their zero-emission and low-noise characteristics, have emerged as a key driver of green innovation. Diverse potential consumer uses, such as urban air taxis, regional commuting, and emergency logistics, introduce high-power, stochastic, and temporally variable loads that significantly affect airport and VTOL site power systems. This review systematically analyzes the impact of electric aircraft on power systems, emphasizing the interplay between consumer-driven charging behavior and consumer electronics technology. It summarizes the challenges and current research status related to charging infrastructure, power supply, and energy management, and finally discusses future development trends.

title: Research on an Intelligent Optimization Algorithm for Indoor Space Design Based on Deep Learning
authors: Wu Binxi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Traditional indoor space design optimization algorithms suffer from insufficient multimodal information fusion, low solution feasibility, and low optimization efficiency. This paper proposes a Multimodal Feature Fusion Indoor Space Optimization Network (MFF-ISON). This algorithm innovatively introduces a spatial semantic attention mechanism to achieve precise cross-modal fusion of floor plans, user requirement text, and environmental parameters. Furthermore, a constraint-aware loss function is designed to incorporate spatial physical and ergonomic constraints into the optimization process, effectively reducing the conflict rate of solutions. To validate the algorithm's performance, an experimental dataset consisting of 5,000 sets of floor plan data and user requirements was constructed. Experiments were conducted using the PyTorch framework on an Intel Core i9-13900K CPU and an NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU. Experimental results comparing a traditional genetic algorithm, a single CNN optimization algorithm, and a basic GAN algorithm show that the MFF-ISON algorithm achieves an average space utilization rate of 89.2%, a 15.6 percentage point improvement over the traditional genetic algorithm. The average comfort score is 92.3 out of 100, 12.7 points higher than the basic GAN algorithm. The algorithm runs for an average of 4.8 minutes, 42.3% less than the single CNN algorithm. The conflict rate is only 2.1%, 6.8 percentage points lower than the lowest value among the compared algorithms. These experimental results demonstrate that the MFF-ISON algorithm significantly improves the practicality and satisfaction of interior space design solutions while maintaining optimization efficiency, providing effective technical support for intelligent optimization of interior space design.

title: Spoken Language Evaluation Through Aienhanced Pronunciation Feedback Systems
authors: Nadira Khalkhmurzayeva, Zarnigor Sobirova, Sevara Muhammadiyeva, Nafisa Norova, Feruza Xasanova, Sohibjon Misirov, Shukurllo Mamatov
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Speech evaluation is one of the significant parts of language learning and assessment, which involves correct pronunciation and fluency analysis. As artificial intelligence has been improved, AI-enhanced pronunciation feedback systems have come into existence to give learners automated, real-time feedback. The current systems tend to use the traditional speech recognition algorithms, which can be unable to face the variability of accents, mispronunciations, and the difference in time of speech patterns and provide incorrect feedback and prevent effective learning of the learner. To cover these shortcomings, the offered approach incorporates Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) as the fundamental approach to the evaluation of pronunciation. DTW allows the accurate alignment of the speech of the learners to the reference utterances based on the temporal differences calculation and the reduction of the distance between the sequences of the speech features. By this method, any changes in speed of speech, intonation, and articulation can be handled effectively and precise feedback can be provided by the system. To evaluate pronunciation accuracy, identify instances of mispronunciation and provide language learners with the corrective recommendations, the proposed DTW-based system is implemented. The results of the experiment show that this method increases the accuracy of feedback, minimizes the errors of alignment, and improves pronunciation abilities of the learners compared to the conventional ones.

title: LLM-Based Bitrate Selection Method for CMR-Supported Audio Codec
authors: Bongsu Jeon, Minsuk Ko, Keyyoung Park, Minho Shin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Recent advances in AI-driven network algorithms have aimed to enhance user experience under diverse network conditions. However, designing and validating optimal AI models tailored to specific network environments and objectives remains a challenge for developers. To address this, frameworks such as netLLM [1], which integrates pretrained large language models (LLMs) with multimodal encoders, network heads, and low-rank adaptation (LoRA) [2], have been proposed and shown to improve performance through offline fine-tuning. In this paper, we extend the concept of netLLM to the bitrate optimization problem of audio codecs supporting Codec Mode Request (CMR). Unlike conventional offline training approaches, we propose an online learning-based bitrate selection method that dynamically adapts to fluctuating real-time communication environments. Our method leverages pretrained LLMs to adjust audio stream bitrates according to network conditions, achieving significant improvements in speech quality, as measured by the E-MODEL R-factor [4], compared to conventional AI models [7]. This study demonstrates an effective approach to audio streaming optimization through online fine-tuning, offering a novel pathway for improving speech quality under variable network conditions.

title: Image Recognition of Textile Healing Effect Based on Transformer-LSTM
authors: Zhirui Rao, Boze Lei
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenge of quantifying cross-modal correlations between textile microstructure and dynamic physiological responses in therapeutic efficacy evaluation, this paper proposes a hybrid model integrating standard Transformer and LSTM. The dual-stream architecture synergistically analyzes static image features and temporal biometric feedback: the main network employs a standard Transformer encoder to extract surface features such as micrometer-scale fiber orientation and pore distribution, while the LSTM branch processes physiological sequences collected during wear. A cross-attention mechanism establishes quantitative mappings between textile structural parameters and physiological indicators. Validated on 12,480 multimodal datasets, the model achieves an F1-score of 0.927 in oven cotton classification and the average F1-score reaches 0.901. This work demonstrates the effectiveness of standard Transformer-LSTM in fusing static and temporal data, providing a scalable solution for intelligent textile efficacy assessment.

title: Research on Energy Management Method for Hydrogen-Powered Intercity EMU Cluster Power System
authors: Yuan Liu, Ying Han, Jianhua Cheng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the deficiencies in operational performance and inconsistent power unit lifespans of hydrogenpowered intercity EMU, this paper proposes a domain-based hierarchical energy management framework. First, to decouple the computational burden and coordinate the hybrid power sources, a single-unit model is employed within the Pontryagin Minimum Principle-Model Predictive Control (PMP-MPC) framework. By optimizing the power split between the PEMFC and the ESS, this layer generates a total power baseline for the cluster, ensuring the SOC of the ESS is maintained within a rational range. Subsequently, inter-unit coordination is achieved through a Remaining Useful Life (RUL)-adaptive power redistribution method based on the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM), By coordinating RUL control, this method ensures balanced load margins across all power units. Results demonstrate that the proposed method enhances system operational performance, optimizes load balancing, and extends overall service life, providing support for the application of the hydrogen-powered intercity EMU.

title: Copper Price Forecasting of COMEX Copper Based on Machine Learning: A Comparative Study of LSTM, ARIMA, and SVR Models
authors: Jing Tang, Bo Li, Jing Wu, Linhai Meng, Xiaobin Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Ten years of COMEX front-month closes (2 506 days) are rolled forward one observation at a time to produce 1 501 out-of-sample one-step forecasts. A single-layer LSTM with 64 hidden units and dropout 0.15 beats the in-house ARIMA(0,1,0) benchmark by 1.5 c/lb RMSE and 19 percentage points in directional accuracy; ε-SVR with an RBF kernel finishes between the two. The neural network keeps prediction error below 10 c/lb even during the 2020-Q2 volatility spike, a period when the linear model exceeds 15 c/lb. Gate-activation traces show that the forget unit discards history once rolling kurtosis exceeds 5.5, letting the cell state adapt to fat-tail shocks without external regressors. The specification satisfies the treasury desk’s runtime limit (38 s per retraining) and 65 % hit-ratio veto, cutting monthly copper VaR by 12 %.

title: Design of Low Voltage Electrical Cabinet for New Energy Vehicle Workshop Based on EPLAN PRO PANEL
authors: Huang Yuting, Ding Mao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid development of new energy vehicles, the competition in the automotive industry is becoming increasingly fierce. In order to implement the concept of "cost saving, cost reduction and efficiency improvement" for internship automotive companies, fully reflect the significant historical significance of "carbon peak and carbon neutrality", and improve the technical level of employees to better test and repair spare parts on site, workshop technical maintenance personnel have decided to build a testing area. Based on the actual needs on site, design a low-voltage electrical cabinet, determine power standards, select electrical components, and design electrical schematics for the types of electrical equipment and power specifications in the repair area. At the same time, use EPLAN PRO PANEL software for three-dimensional layout of the electrical cabinet, optimize wiring and calculate slot fullness. Use machine vision technology to inspect the completed electrical cabinet and verify the consistency between the electrical schematic and the three-dimensional layout space.

title: DiffCNN: A Neural Network-based Diffusion Model for Identification and Quantification of Cracks in Concrete Bridges
authors: Vijay Prakash, Carl James Debono, Dylan Seychell, Muhammad Ali Musarat, Ruben Paul Borg, Wei Ding, Jiangpeng Shu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Concrete bridges suffer degradation and damage over time, eventually leading to failure and collapse. Therefore, monitoring these structures is necessary to identify damage early and plan timely maintenance to avoid structural failures. This paper presents a neural network-based diffusion model (DiffCNN) to identify and quantify cracks in concrete bridges' deck, wall, and pavement using both transfer learning (TL) and fully trained (FT) models. The noise control and segmentation outcome are improved with a Gaussian distribution, a CNNbased architecture, and a diffusion module. Experiments on the SDNET2018 dataset show that the proposed DiffCNN model achieves a crack detection accuracy of 96.85% on the bridge deck images, 94.61% on the bridge wall images, and 99.12% on the bridge pavement images in the FT mode. Furthermore, in the TL mode, the model achieved a crack detection accuracy of 99.71% on the bridge deck images, 99.62% on the bridge wall images, and 99.93% on the bridge pavement images, outperforming traditional Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) architectures and vision transformers.

title: Decision Making Assistance for Electricity Spot Market Based on CNN-XGBoost Integrated Algorithm
authors: Wenjie Fan, Yue Yang, Guiyang Tian
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In the electricity spot market, the use of information technology intelligent algorithms to analyze and predict the constantly updated massive data, obtain its inherent correlation to reveal changes in market supply and demand, is a key factor for market entities to maintain their core competitiveness. This article starts from market analysis, user load forecasting, and spot market clearing electricity prices, and combines machine learning algorithms to conduct research on spot market clearing electricity prices and short-term user load forecasting. This article uses a grid search parameter self optimization algorithm to update the hyperparameters of the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model, and builds a fully connected neural network electricity price prediction model with multiple hidden layers by combining Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) algorithm. The effectiveness and accuracy of this CNN-XGBoost integrated algorithm has been demonstrated in simulation examples.

title: Method for Error State Assessment of Current Transformers Based on Multimodal Global-Local Fusion Network
authors: Shaojun Wu, Banghai Yu, Songhui Zhang, Haoran Sui, Changxiao Zhang, Chunhui Wang, Yekui Tan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In power system safe operation, error state assessment of current transformers (CTs) is crucial for accurate measurement and reliable protection. Addressing limitations in existing research-foreign single-modal analysis fails to capture error correlation characteristics, while domestic fusion methods lack synchronous extraction of global and local features, leading to insufficient assessment accuracy under complex conditions-this paper proposes an error state assessment method based on a Multimodal Global-Local Fusion Network (MG-LFNet). First, one-dimensional current time-series data is converted into multimodal two-dimensional image data via GAF, MTF, and RPM algorithms. Then, global operation rules and local defect features are synchronously extracted, followed by accurate error state assessment through multimodal feature fusion and an SVM classifier. Experimental results show the method achieves 99.67 % accuracy and 0.998 AUC value; even under 5 dB high-noise environment, accuracy remains 95.2 % with a standard deviation of only 0.21 %. Its comprehensive performance outperforms mainstream methods, meeting real-time online assessment needs of CT error states under complex working conditions.

title: Dendritic-Spine-Inspired Structural Optimization in a Lightweight Bowel Sound Analysis System for On-Device Colorectal Cancer Screening
authors: Haojie Zhang, Enze Li, Yang Tan, Lin Shen, Kun Qian, Jing Li, Bin Hu, Björn W. Schuller, Yoshiharu Yamamoto
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This study presents a non-invasive AI-assisted Colorectal Cancer (CRC) screening system based on Bowel Sound (BS) analysis. A portable BS acquisition device is integrated with a dendritic-spine-inspired lightweight neural model that mimics biological synaptic pruning to remove redundant parameters while preserving key diagnostic features. The pruned model achieves high accuracy with minimal computational and memory costs, supporting on-device inference on microcontroller. Based on this model, we develop a complete device and system capable of efficient BS screening, demonstrating strong practicality for accessible CRC risk assessment.

title: Detection of Low Attachment and High Use of Safety Ropes Based on Mmpose+Qwen2-VL Model
authors: Xiaona Jiang, Gaoyang Zhang, Dongmei Ma, Tianguang Yang, Yu Zhang, Feng Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In the field of safety monitoring for high-altitude power operations, traditional methods for detecting violations such as low attachment and high use of safety ropes suffer from insufficient accuracy and low efficiency, leading to the risk of falls from heights for workers. To address this, we propose a collaborative detection technique based on mmpose and Qwen2VL multimodal large model. By employing a “mmpose detection + Qwen2-VL model inference” approach, the accuracy of detecting low attachment and high use of safety ropes is improved. This research mainly includes three aspects: Firstly, we constructed a high-precision labeled dataset containing 4000 frames of images, annotating key points of the human body and safety rope, including both compliant and non-compliant scenarios in highaltitude operations. Then, the accuracy of key point localization is improved by fine-tuning the HRNet model in mmpose. Finally, through the collaboration of mmpose and Qwen2-VL models, mmpose extracts the coordinates of the human waist and the endpoints of the safety rope, and Qwen2-VL is used for violation determination based on spatial relationship inference. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves precision, recall, and F 1 scores of 81.3 %, 80.8 %, and 81.05 %, respectively, in the detection task of low attachment and high use of safety ropes, significantly outperforming the single model. This method realizes automated and high-precision detection of violations, providing reliable technical support for the safety supervision of high-altitude power operations.

title: Signal Propagation and Energy Consumption in Multiband Wi-Fi: A Case Study on Mobile Devices
authors: João Victor L. De Souza, Wesley B. Conde, Gustavo M. Torres, Janislley O. De Sousa, Higo Ferreira Albuquerque, David Alan De O. Ferreira
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This work evaluates signal fading and energy consumption of a mobile device in multiband Wi-Fi networks using an Android hotspot. Experiments were conducted in an indoor environment using the 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, and 6 GHz bands. The analysis included Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) measurements and propagation modeling based on the log- normal shadowing and Nakagami-m distributions, characterizing both large-scale and small-scale fading. The results show that higher frequency bands exhibit increased signal attenuation and more severe multipath fading, with Nakagami-m values dropping below 2.5 at longer distances. In contrast, the 2.4 GHz band maintained greater robustness, with m ≈ 13.5 at 15 m. Additionally, electrical current and power consumption increased proportionally with frequency, reaching up to 240 mA and 1047 mW in the 6 GHz band. These findings highlight a trade-off between communication performance and energy efficiency that must be considered when deploying mobile solutions using high-frequency Wi-Fi technologies for IoT applications.

title: SkyShield: A Smart Drone Swarm for Real-Time UAV Identification and Threat Mitigation
authors: Neeraj Raghav V R, Manaswini K, Mohithaa K, Shre Haaraan A, Venkateshkumar M
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The rapid growth in UAV deployment has created formidable tasks in securing restricted airspace around militarily sensitive areas, airports, and areas of public gatherings. Conventional counter-drone systems cannot provide adaptive and real-time responses in challenging environments. SkyShield proposes an autonomous multi-UAV defense architecture with radar-based sensing, secure communication, blockchain-based identity verification, and AI-driven payload analysis. In particular, the system designs a central decision-making drone, ESP32-CAMbased support drones, and a Flask-powered ground station interconnected through a private Ethereum blockchain. The system guarantees reliable UAV identification via dual mode detection with simulation radar and encrypted GPS signals. For threat assessment, the YOLOv8 model is used to achieve real-time payload classification. Extensive experimental results have verified an end-to-end response time around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 ~\mathrm{s}, 92 \%$</tex> radar detection accuracy, and an F1-score of 0.85 for AI-based classification. As such, it further confirms that SkyShield provides a scalable and efficient solution to secure UAV defense with potential future improvement for on-site radar deployment and swarm collaboration.

title: An Intelligent Online Monitoring Method Based on the VQ-VAE Model
authors: Yuxuan Liu, Yan Fang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of intelligent manufacturing and information technologies, industrial systems are becoming increasingly dependent on real-time monitoring and data analysis. Traditional monitoring approaches typically rely on offline data processing, which limits their ability to respond promptly to system state variations and to extract latent features from large-scale time-series data. To address these challenges, this study proposes an intelligent monitoring method based on the Vector Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE). The proposed approach enables automatic feature learning and state prediction directly from raw sensor signals, eliminating the need for complex time-, frequency-, or time-frequency-domain feature engineering commonly required in conventional models. This greatly improves computational efficiency and model generalization capability. Experimental results based on an online monitoring platform demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior real-time performance, stability, and prediction accuracy, providing an efficient and scalable solution for intelligent monitoring and data-driven decision-making in complex systems.

title: A Synergistic Cyber-Physical Framework for Motorcyclist Safety: Integrating Automated SOS, Heads-Up Navigation, and Hands-Free Communication
authors: M Yuva Praneeth, Murarisetty V Sai Kartheek, Sita Devi Bharatula
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The persistent safety challenges faced by motorcyclists, including delayed post-accident aid, cognitive distraction from navigation, and unsafe call handling, demand an integrated technological intervention. Current solutions often address these issues in isolation, failing to provide a comprehensive safety net. This paper introduces the architecture of a smart helmet, an IoT-driven system engineered to create a cohesive and secure riding environment by holistically addressing these critical risks. The proposed system uses an inertial measurement unit for autonomous accident detection, which automatically dispatches the rider’s GPS location to designated contacts. To mitigate navigational distractions, turn-by-turn directions are projected within the rider’s field of view. The helmet also integrates a microphone and speaker module, enabling hands-free call management via a Bluetooth link. By amalgamating these functionalities into a single, wearable device, this work presents a significant step toward proactive motorcyclist safety. The architecture ensures that critical safety features—automated emergency alerts, non-intrusive navigation, and secure communication—operate in synergy, thereby enhancing rider awareness and significantly reducing response time in emergency scenarios.

title: DrinkSync: A Sustainable, Universally Compatible Hydration Tracking System
authors: Karim Smires, Kareem Kholaif, Mohammad Daoud, Sasan Haghani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper presents DrinkSync, a low-cost and sustainable hydration tracking system designed to transform any reusable bottle into a smart device. The system integrates a load cell with HX711 amplification and an MPU6050 inertial sensor controlled by an Arduino Nano, transmitting data via Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) to an Android application. The app provides real-time visualization, goal tracking, and gamified engagement. Experimental evaluation across varying container geometries demonstrated sub-gram accuracy (0.05 g mean error) and BLE latency below 300 ms. Power profiling indicates a theoretical multi-day runtime of 38–68 h, while the total bill-of-materials (BOM) cost remains roughly $24. DrinkSync promotes sustainability through reusable hardware, open-source firmware, and universal compatibility, offering an affordable, reproducible solution for personal and clinical hydration monitoring.

title: Research on audio signal preprocessing based on neural network
authors: Li Yingtong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the influence of preprocess on the performance of DNN. In this paper, we carry out a series of preliminary tests, including time domain transform, log amplitude compression, frequency-weighting and scaling. This paper also presents a modified MFCC _ E. Compared with MFCC, MFCC _ E can save more computation effort by about 50 percent and can be easily realized in hardware.

title: Adaptive Semantic Gates for Reliable Multi-Agent LLMs
authors: Lucas G. M. Castro, Myke D. M. Valadão, Waldir S. S. Júnior, Celso B. Carvalho
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: LLM-based multi-agent systems have recently advanced collaborative reasoning, planning, and tool use, yet remain fragile under real-world execution. Prior studies, including recent analyses of why multi-agent LLM systems fail, consistently report coordination breakdowns, unsafe actions, and violations of system-level constraints, while existing defenses rely on heavy alignment methods or static rule-based filters that do not generalize across domains. To address this gap, we introduce ASG, a lightweight, plug-and-play mechanism that monitors semantic drift in real time and selectively blocks high-risk actions during multi-agent execution. We evaluate ASG across symbolic tasks, real-world applications, and multi-step workflows using five configurations and eight seeds. In stable environments, ASG-enhanced agents achieve a team success rate of 91.7% with 26 prevented unsafe actions, demonstrating measurable robustness improvements over traditional pipelines. Under stress-test conditions with noisy states and long-horizon workflows, ASG shifts to a safety-first regime, preventing 33 destructive or contradictory actions while matching or exceeding the stability of non-defensive baselines. These findings show that adaptive gating directly addresses the failure modes highlighted in recent multi-agent analyses and offers a practical, domain-general pathway toward more reliable LLM-based systems.

title: Comparative Study on Power Converter Topologies for Multilevel Converter-Based Sonar Power Amplifiers
authors: Aswini Narayanan, V N Panchalai
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Active Sonar Transmitter System uses Power Amplifiers (PA) to drive electroacoustic transducer elements with the amplified sonar control signals. The underwater electro acoustic projector is a complex load and is resistive at resonant frequencies and reactive on either side of the resonant frequencies. Multiple stages of power conversion is required to optimize the Sonar Power Amplifier and the Transducer interface. Multilevel Converter Topologies are gaining popularity in the high power SPA design as it requires high voltage and current drive output which helps in long range detection. In this paper, a comparative study on few suitable topologies for high power SPA is presented. Analysis on each topology based on the system requirement parameter and advantages and disadvantages of each topology is explained. Output Power, Efficiency, THD, and Dynamic range of operation are major parameters considered here for the comparison. Simulation study of each topology is done using the Simulink/Matlab and salient results are presented.

title: High-Slot-Loaded Two-Element Patch Antenna Arrays: A Performance Comparison for 5G Applications
authors: Barsa Panda, Debasis Mishra, Ashish Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This letter investigates the performance and requirements of High gain microstrip patch antennas for Sub-6 GHz 5G applications. Here the rectangular patch antenna is gradually modified with 3-different techniques: (i) Edge-cut single rectangular patch antenna (ii) Wheel shape slot loaded on the radiating patches (iii) Staircase-shape two element rectangular patch antenna array. This paper discussed 4 different rectangular microstrip patch antenna (RMPA) designs (Design - 1, 2, 3, 4) for Fifth generation communication system. The aim of the Wheel shape structure is to achieve wide bandwidth and antenna gain; Edge truncation technique helps to improve impedance matching and Staircase-shape structure helps to enhance bandwidth as well as achieves compactness of the antenna. All these antennas are convenient for Sub-6GHz 5G- NR band n79 (4.4 GHz to 5 GHz). All these structures are designed and simulated in ANSYS HFSS 2019 software.

title: Early Damage Detection in Bridges Using Magnetostrictive Sensors Under Traffic Loading Scenarios
authors: Cherosree Dolui, Debabrata Roy
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Bridges play a vital role in transportation infrastructure, yet they are vulnerable to both natural hazards and overloading conditions that often lead to progressive deterioration or catastrophic failure. Traditional inspection-based maintenance practices are labor-intensive and cannot capture hidden or progressive internal damage. This study proposes a continuous structural health monitoring approach using a magnetostrictive sensor modeled in COMSOL Multiphysics. A scaled-down bridge model was simulated under three conditions: no loading, two trucks, and ten trucks. Vibration data extracted from the bridge were provided as input to a cantilever-based MsS model, where electrical output was analyzed. The unloaded case produced no electrical output, while the two-truck case yielded a clean voltage waveform of 5.4 V. Under the ten-truck scenario, a higher output of 15.2 V was observed but accompanied by significant waveform spikes, indicating stress-induced internal damage. The study demonstrates the feasibility of magnetostrictive sensors for real-time, battery-free monitoring of bridges.

title: AI-Driven Optimization of EV Battery Charging
authors: R Thamizharasan, Ramathilagam K, R Mythili, Sukitha D, Sivakarthikeyan G
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This project presents an AI-based electric vehicle (EV) battery charging system utilizing a crossover renewable energy arrangement, comprising of a 630 W photovoltaic (PV) cluster and a three-phase wind energy system. The AC yield from the wind turbine is changed over to DC and combined with the PV control to supply a DC-DC converter. An Artificial Neural Network (ANN) (ANN)-based Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) controller creates ideal exchanging signals for the converter, maximizing energy extraction from both sources beneath changing natural conditions. The directed DC yield is utilized to securely and effectively charge a 48 V, 31.25 V EV battery. MATLAB/Simulink recreations illustrate that the ANN-MPPT controller successfully makes strides energy collecting while guaranteeing a steady battery charging profile, highlighting the potential of AI-integrated renewable energy for economical EV charging.

title: Integrated Blockchain and Machine Learning Framework for Polystyrene Waste Traceability
authors: Hamza Ibrahim, Prosper Uzoma Ahakonye, Precious Nwadiuto Ahakonye, Won Jae Ryu, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The management of expanded and extruded polystyrene (EPS/XPS) waste presents a significant environmental challenge due to limited recyclability and the generation of microplastics. Conventional waste management systems often lack transparency, data interoperability, and operational efficiency. This study introduces a smart waste management framework that integrates Internet of Things (IoT) sensing, Machine Learning (ML), and blockchain technologies to enable traceable, data-driven energy recovery. The proposed system employs PureChain, a custom permissioned blockchain designed for the immutable recording of data from IoT-enabled smart bins equipped with fill-level, temperature, and waste-type sensors. Performance evaluation demonstrated that PureChain achieved a throughput of 2,847 transactions per second, a latency of 0.3 s, and an energy consumption of 0.02 kWh per transaction. For predictive analytics, the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model achieved the highest forecasting accuracy of 94.8% with a coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.947. In contrast, the XGBoost model maintained balanced accuracy of 92.8% and superior computational efficiency. The integration of PureChain and ML creates a closed-loop, intelligent system that enhances data traceability, optimizes waste-collection logistics, and enables a verifiable circular economy for EPS/XPS waste management.

title: Real-Time Controller Implementation Using dSPACE RTI1202 and MATLAB/Simulink: Architecture, Integration, and Applications
authors: Ashok Kumar, Praveen Tripathy, Sreenath J.G, Ravindranath Adda
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic framework for the implementation of real-time control algorithms using the dSPACE RTI-1202 rapid prototyping system integrated with MATLAB/Simulink and control-desk. The proposed workflow covers model-based design, code generation using real-time interface blocks, and automatic deployment onto the DS1202 hardware. Hardware configuration procedures for pulse width modulation (PWM) generation, analog-to-digital/digital-to-analog converter sampling, and general-purpose input output (GPIO) interfacing are explained in detail, along with the integration of controller logic within the Simulink environment. Real-time monitoring, parameter tuning, and data logging through control-desk are demonstrated for efficient experimental validation. To evaluate the system's performance, a notch filter-based PLL is implemented for grid synchronisation under distorted voltage conditions. The proposed setup precisely estimates the grid frequency and phase angle. The results confirm the high-speed processing, precise timing, and robust real-time capability of the dSPACE platform for advanced control and power electronics applications. This work aims to serve as a foundational guide for researchers and engineers seeking to develop and test real-time digital controllers using the dSPACE RTI1202 system.

title: Target Detection using Optimized AIRBORNE SAR System in Stripmap mode
authors: Pranshu Upadhyay, Sandeep Kumar, Manisha Bharti, Kritika Upadhyay
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Nowadays, an enormous number of military applications use synthetic aperture radar (SAR), specifically AIRBORNE SAR. Conventional airborne SAR systems are expensive, sophisticated, and have a high volume. It cannot be repeatedly installed for various applications. In this paper, utilizing an optimal airborne SAR system operating in stripmap mode, object detection has been accomplished. Highly accurate and precise object detection is being carried out with the aid of the range migration algorithm and the back-propagation algorithm. In addition to object detection, factors like environmental conditions that hamper the Airborne SAR system's ability to create images are examined.

title: Application of Multimodal Artificial Intelligence Fusion Model in Individualized Prediction of Depression
authors: Jianhao Zeng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The individualized prediction of depression aims to predict an individual's risk of developing depression and related evaluation indicators by analyzing data such as individual behavior, emotional states, physiological signals, and social environment. Online platforms for individualized depression prediction possess massive user data, which brings the risk of user privacy leakage. As an effective means to mitigate this risk, differential privacy machine learning needs to further consider users' privacy demands for different data attributes. Moreover, existing differential privacy methods with average privacy budget allocation inject noise into the model, leading to a decline in model performance. To address the above issues, an individualized depression prediction method with adaptive privacy budget allocation (APBA-DP) is proposed. Firstly, attributes are classified according to users' privacy preferences, and personalized privacy budgets are allocated to attributes using information entropy. Then, an attribute mapping layer is established for the individualized depression prediction model, and differential privacy protection is implemented based on the personalized privacy budgets. Experimental results on the depression-related datasets (e.g., PHQ-9 related datasets and MDD diagnostic datasets) show that, under a certain privacy protection strength, the APBA-DP algorithm improves the accuracy by 2.3% - 4.4% compared with traditional differential privacy algorithms. Meanwhile, the success rate of membership inference attacks against it is reduced by an average of 14.7% and 12.5% compared with the model without differential privacy protection.

title: Electro-Thermal Coupling Modelling for Liquid Metal Battery Based on Equivalent Circuit Model
authors: Liming Tao, Lei Fan, Lingrong Pang, Mingwei Wang, Aji Hu, Aonan Li, Zhengwei Guo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper develops an efficient electro-thermal coupling model for a 200 Ah Li <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mid \text{Bi}$</tex> liquid metal battery, based on an equivalent circuit approach combined with a lumpedparameter thermal model. The electrical parameters, including state-of-charge dependent ohmic and polarization resistances, are characterized via hybrid pulse power characterization tests. Simulation results indicate that ohmic polarization dominates irreversible heat generation, while reversible entropy change remains the primary driver of temperature variation. During cycling, the battery temperature fluctuates between approximately 519 °C and 540 °C under 0.1 C conditions, and between about 520 °C and 557 °C under 0.2 C conditions. The proposed model balances computational efficiency with predictive capability, offering a practical tool for analyzing thermal behavior and supporting the design of thermal management systems for liquid metal battery modules.

title: Flip-flop resource expansion method for FPGAs using latches created on look-up tables
authors: Ayumu Ogura, Minoru Watanabe, Nobuya Watanabe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Applications for pipeline processing or image processing using the latest field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) often require a number of delay-type flip-flops (D-FFs). To increase the flip-flops resource, this paper proposes a flip-flop resource expansion method for current FPGAs using primary and secondary latches created on look-up tables as additional D-FFs. However, this method causes an increase in clock skew because the clock signal must be supplied to the LUT using general-purpose programmable wiring. To address this problem, the additional D-FFs consisting of primary and secondary latches use a two-phase clock signal. This paper presents the demonstration results for a large shift register circuit using this proposed method.

title: Research on Lightweight Transformer for Hybrid Force-Position Control of Flexible Manipulators
authors: Yinli Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenges of insufficient robustness of traditional algorithms for hybrid force-position control of flexible manipulators and the poor real-time performance of standard Transformer models, this paper designs a lightweight Transformer model that integrates channel pruning, quantization compression, and local attention. An experimental platform based on PyTorch and PyBullet is constructed to verify its performance. Simulation experiments using a 6-DOF flexible robotic arm compared traditional PID, fuzzy PID, and standard Transformer algorithms. In static scenarios, the lightweight Transformer achieved a static position error of 0.12mm, an 85.9% reduction compared to the traditional PID (0.85mm), and a static force error of 0.08N, a 75% reduction compared to the fuzzy PID (0.32N). In dynamic scenarios, the dynamic position error was 0.18mm, an 18.2% reduction compared to the standard Transformer (0.22mm), and the recovery time after a 5N disturbance was 0.3s, a 25% reduction compared to the standard Transformer (0.4s). In terms of model efficiency, the parameter count was 1.2MParams, an 85.9% reduction compared to the standard Transformer (8.5MParams), and the CPU inference latency was 8.1ms, meeting 200Hz real-time control requirements. The results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a coordinated optimization of accuracy, robustness, and real-time performance, making it suitable for scenarios such as precision electronic assembly.

title: Construction and Application of a Digital Platform for Carbon Footprint of Electrical Equipment Based on Time-sharing Dynamic Power Carbon Emission Factors
authors: Sijue Wang, Jianqiang Dong, Juanyu Liu, Xinyue Liu, Xiaojing Liu, Zhiyi Hu, Ruichao Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: At present, when the carbon neutrality strategy is steadily advancing, the low-carbon development of the power equipment industry has attracted much attention. This paper proposes a carbon footprint spatio-temporal dynamic coupling accounting model based on the mapping rules of the full life cycle stage in response to the accounting deviation caused by the current reliance on static power carbon factors, and develops a dedicated carbon footprint digital platform for electrical equipment. This platform innovatively adopts a dual-track accounting framework of time-varying dynamic factors and fixed factors, supporting parallel computing and result comparison. The empirical study of power transmission cables shows that there is a systematic deviation in dynamic accounting compared with the traditional method. The research results aim to provide full-chain technical support for the electrical equipment industry to cope with new environmental regulations such as the EU carbon tariff, and effectively enhance the international competitiveness of the industry in low-carbon development by improving the accuracy of carbon accounting.

title: Efficient Distributed TDMA Scheduling and Graph Construction in Heterogeneous Wireless Sensor Networks with Enhanced Aggregation and Energy Optimizations
authors: Niranjan L, Ananth Kumar M. S, Sreekantha B, R.Bhargava Rama Gowd, Shilpa A N, Sridhar N
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSNs) are widely employed for environmental monitoring and data collection tasks. These networks consist of spatially distributed sensor nodes that gather data such as temperature, humidity, pressure, and light intensity, which is then relayed to a central node known as the sink or base station. In heterogeneous (distributed) networks, where diverse sensor types coexist, data aggregation poses unique challenges compared to homogeneous (uniform) networks with identical node types. After deployment, a routing structure—typically a tree rooted at the sink—is established to facilitate data forwarding. However, effective aggregation in distributed settings often requires nodes to consider multiple potential parents and handle varying data types, which complicates tree formation and scheduling. To address these complexities, we present an enhanced method that integrates distributed tree construction with communication scheduling. Our approach improves data aggregation efficiency, reduces scheduling delays, and conserves energy. The results also highlight the increased coordination overhead needed for managing distributed networks in contrast to uniform configurations.

title: Emotion-Aware Smart Home Automation Based on the eBICA Model
authors: Masaaki Yamauchi, Yiyuan Liang, Hiroko Hara, Hideyuki Shimonishi, Masayuki Murata
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Smart home automation that adapts to a user’s emotional state can enhance psychological safety in daily living environments. This study proposes an emotion-aware automation framework guided by the emotional Biologically Inspired Cognitive Architecture (eBICA), which integrates appraisal, somatic responses, and behavior selection. We conducted a proof-of- concept experiment in a pseudo-smart-home environment, where participants were exposed to an anxiety-inducing event followed by a comfort-inducing automation. State anxiety (STAI-S) was measured throughout the task sequence. The results showed a significant reduction in STAI-S immediately after introducing the avoidance automation, demonstrating that emotion-based control can effectively promote psychological safety. Furthermore, an analysis of individual characteristics suggested that personality and anxiety-related traits modulate the degree of relief, indicating the potential for personalized emotion-adaptive automation. Overall, this study provides empirical evidence that eBICA-based emotional control can function effectively in smart home environments and offers a foundation for next-generation affective home automation systems.

title: Intelligent EV Charging Algorithm
authors: Anirudha S. Marothiya, Sarthak R. Nagose, Purva D. Dahake, Saurabh T. Chandankhede, Vedant A. Mahajan, Kirti H. Madan, Harshit S. Dalvi
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This research presents the Smart Charging for Electric Vehicles (SCFEV) algorithm, engineered to enhance electric vehicle (EV) charging by integrating solar photovoltaic (PV) panels, battery storage systems (BSS), and grid electricity. Utilizing a ZMPT101B AC voltage sensor and a DC voltage sensor, the algorithm dynamically optimizes charging decisions by ranking solar PV power (S2V mode) when available, seamlessly transitioning to BSS (B2V mode) or grid (G2V mode) based on real-time grid conditions, BSS state of charge (SOC), and energy availability. Validated through extensive testing, the SCFEV algorithm showcases reliable decision-making, effectively maximizing renewable energy use, minimizing grid dependence during peak demand, and leveraging grid power primarily during off-peak hours. The results confirm its adaptability across various conditions, consistently aligning with anticipated charging source outcomes. By optimizing solar energy and BSS utilization, the algorithm reduces operational costs and grid reliance. This study offers a scalable, intelligent framework for EV charging, contributing valuable insights to the research community and industry for advancing renewable energy-driven systems and sustainable transportation infrastructure.

title: Research on Complex Multi Objective Systems Based on Intelligent Feedback Optimization Framework and Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Binglin Cheng, Wenchao Wang, Chaopan Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In complex systems characterized by nonlinear coupling and dynamic constraints, multi-objective optimization is the core challenge of intelligent decision-making. Traditional methods are difficult to meet the requirements due to limited modeling capabilities and poor algorithm convergence. Therefore, this article proposes the MIND-F intelligent feedback optimization framework to solve problems such as target conflicts and dynamic changes in constraints in high-dimensional systems. The framework includes three layers of mechanisms: multi-objective coupling modeling, intelligent evolutionary optimization, and feedback regulation. Global search and local optimization are achieved through improved genetic algorithms, and dynamic constraint perception is introduced to enhance the system's anti-interference ability. Experiments have shown that this framework outperforms traditional algorithms such as NSGA-II in terms of convergence efficiency and feasible solution ratio. It exhibits strong robustness under dynamic disturbances and provides transferable modeling and algorithm solutions for complex system optimization.
